subject: [PATCH v2 4/5] btrfs: make inode->outstanding_extents a u64

reviews.author: Filipe Manana
reviews.summary: Reviewer requested the addition of extended assertions in the code to provide more informative error messages when certain conditions are not met.
reviews.raw_body:
Please use extended assertions that print the checked values, so that
we can have useful output to debug if they fail.

Thanks.

2026-04-06.patch_summary: This patch updates the data type of the 'outstanding_extents' field in the Btrfs inode structure from an unsigned integer (u32) to a 64-bit unsigned integer (u64). This change is motivated by the fact that the maximum file size is limited by LLONG_MAX, which is approximately 2^63. As a result, the maximum extent size computation in Btrfs has always been bounded above by this value, making it necessary to use a larger data type to accurately represent this value. The patch also updates several functions and variables to work with the new u64 data type.
